project_subtitle: Your companion for returning to missed prayers and fasts, with structure, privacy, and quiet persistence.
why_body: Awdah was built to make qadaa feel less like a burden and more like a steady practice. The goal is a calm, private experience that respects the weight of the journey and helps you stay consistent, without pressure.
who_body: Whether you're returning after years away or tracking a small backlog of missed days, Awdah gives you the structure and clarity to move forward at your own pace.

features.title: Salah Tracking
features.body: Track your missed obligatory prayers with a clear picture of where you stand and how far you've come.

features.title: Sawm Tracking
features.body: Log missed Ramadan fasts with dedicated progress tracking and a clear view of what remains.

features.title: Hijri First
features.body: Built around the Islamic calendar, with accurate dual-date support for every entry you make.

features.title: Bilingual + RTL
features.body: Designed for Arabic and English from the ground up, with full right-to-left support built in, not bolted on.

features.title: Privacy First
features.body: Minimal data collection by design. Your data stays yours, with clear export and deletion controls always available.

features.title: Learn Hub
features.body: Reference content explaining the rulings, terminology, and thinking behind how qadaa tracking works in Awdah.

team.name: Amgad Mahmoud
team.bio:
Awdah came from a personal place, a need for something structured and calm that could hold the weight of a spiritual journey without making it feel heavier. So I built one.

I'm a cloud engineer delivering cloud-native digital transformation projects on AWS, working across multi-account platform integration, IaC automation with CDKTF and AWS CDK, and CI/CD pipelines with integrated security gates. My work covers the full delivery stack: multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure, cloud automation, observability engineering, and cost governance. I care about clean architecture, scalable system design, and building things that are intentional by nature.
